signals.title: Code of Practice on Marking and Labelling AI-Generated Content
signals.summary: The European Commission published the first draft of the Code of Practice on marking and labelling AI-generated content, aligning with Article 50 of the AI Act. This initiative mandates providers to mark AI-generated content to ensure transparency.
key_provisions: Providers of generative AI systems must mark AI-generated content.
Deployers using generative AI for professional purposes must label deepfakes and AI-generated texts.

signals.title: Formal Proceedings Against X Under the Digital Services Act
signals.summary: The European Commission opened formal proceedings to assess whether X (formerly Twitter) has breached the DSA in areas including risk management, content moderation, and advertising transparency.
key_provisions: Potential inadequacies in content moderation practices.
Insufficient transparency in advertising and data access.

signals.title: Public Consultation on Transparency Reporting Under the DSA
signals.summary: The European Commission initiated a public consultation to gather feedback on the Implementing Regulation concerning templates for transparency reports required under the DSA.
key_provisions: Providers must publish periodic transparency reports.
Reports must include data on orders from authorities and performance of automated moderation systems.

signals.title: Public Consultation on the DSA Transparency Database
signals.summary: The European Commission launched a public consultation on the DSA Transparency Database, which collects statements from online platforms regarding content moderation decisions.
key_provisions: Platforms must submit statements explaining reasons for content removal.
The database aims to provide public insights into the fight against illegal content online.

signals.title: Draft Guidelines on Protection of Minors Online Under the DSA
signals.summary: The European Commission published draft guidelines to create a safer online environment for children, supporting platforms in ensuring high levels of privacy, safety, and security for minors.
key_provisions: Default private settings for children's accounts.

signals.title: Consultation on Declaration Functionality for Media Service Providers
signals.summary: The European Commission initiated a consultation to gather feedback for guidelines on implementing the declaration functionality for media service providers, as outlined in the European Media Freedom Act.
key_provisions: Very large online platforms must implement functionalities allowing media service providers to declare their status.
Aims to safeguard media content from unjustified content moderation.

signals.title: Research API for DSA Transparency Database
signals.summary: The European Commission released a Research API to facilitate programmatic analysis of data within the DSA Transparency Database, enhancing research capabilities into platforms' content moderation practices.
